behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, because this is a static lint rule over template text. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected since behavior text is "static prompt-template lint check" and does not require module handoff beyond the linter function; System rejected (no real OS/network/subprocess dependency); Agentic/Workflow rejected (no model call or agent steps). FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative template strings (including copied phase instructions from SKILL.md) into the lint rule function and assert reject/accept outcomes.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure appears when template + interpolation + fixture wiring compose the final prompt. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration-brake): (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required beyond in-process harness assembly, so not System; (b) no AI output quality dependence, so not Agentic; (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Unit rejected because behavior text requires "fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness." FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual prompt-construction pipeline with real fixtures/interpolation and assert the final assembled prompt omits phase-specific leaked instructions.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, since this is about module/file loading and invocation wiring. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration-brake): (a) local file reads are sufficient; no real external OS/network/subprocess boundary requiring System; (b) behavior text focuses on source-of-truth wiring ("loads skill context from plugin/skill files"), not model judgment quality, so Agentic rejected; (c) no multi-agentic sequence, so Workflow rejected. Unit rejected because the failure is at module handoff (loader -> invocation). FINAL: Integration.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation setup with actual plugin/skill files and assert the resolved context comes from those files (not hard-coded prompt constants), e.g., by using sentinel content in SKILL.md.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because this checks harness parameterization + skill-file variant switching while prompt text is constant. PASS2-AUDIT (Integration-brake): (a) no required real network/subprocess; local filesystem variant swap is enough, so not System; (b) this behavior is about execution setup capability, not correctness of model output content, so Agentic rejected; (c) no multi-agentic chain required, so Workflow rejected. Unit rejected due cross-module/file orchestration requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness twice with identical user prompt text while toggling skill path to normal vs degraded/missing variant; assert invocation succeeds and prompt text is unchanged across runs.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, because pass/fail depends on what the live model does with changed skill context under the same prompt. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected by behavior text "behavioral test fails" after skill degradation; a stubbed model could hide this (deterministic-assertion trap). Workflow considered but rejected: text describes one behavioral outcome under one prompt, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence. System is lower than Agentic and insufficient for model-judgment regressions. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model calls with unchanged user prompt against baseline vs degraded SKILL.md, and assert degraded variant causes structural behavioral failure while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Integration, because failure is ordering across modules (model client -> artifact writer -> parser). PASS2-AUDIT (Integration-brake): (a) no need for real external OS/network/subprocess; local in-process wiring with filesystem artifact is sufficient, so not System; (b) no dependence on model output quality/decision, so not Agentic; (c) no multi-agentic chain, so not Workflow. Unit rejected because this is an interaction-order boundary, not isolated function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke harness with a controlled model response and a parser that can fail; assert raw response artifact is written first and remains available even when parsing/extraction errors.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Unit, as this is assertion logic semantics. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected because behavior text targets assertion rules themselves, not cross-module interaction; System rejected (no real infra); Agentic rejected (can validate with fixed fixtures, no model-quality dependency); Workflow rejected (no multi-step agent pipeline). FINAL: Unit.
behaviors.test_description: Test assertion helpers with crafted outputs that pass keywords but violate schema/role placement, and verify structural checks fail while valid structure passes.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional: Agentic, explicitly required by "Under live model execution." PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected because mocking can hide real model outputs where keyword checks pass but structure regresses; this is LLM-output-dependent. Workflow considered but rejected: behavior text asks for one live execution comparison (structural vs keyword-only), not multiple agentic steps in sequence. FINAL: Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Execute live model tests and compare outcomes of structural assertions vs keyword-only assertions on the same responses; verify at least one regression is caught only by structural checks.
